title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the acceleration, we need to consider the change in velocity and the distance over which this change occurs. The care package starts with a speed of $$54$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ and comes to a stop over a distance of $$3.0$$ $$m$$.

title: Using the Kinematic Equation
text: Recall the kinematic equation: $${v_f}^2={v_i}^2+2a d$$, where $$v_f$$ is the final velocity, $$v_i$$ is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and $$d$$ is the distance.

title: Identifying Known Values
text: In this problem, the final velocity $$v_f$$ is $$0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ (since the package stops), the initial velocity $$v_i$$ is $$54$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, and the distance $$d$$ is $$3.0$$ $$m$$.

title: Solving for Acceleration
text: Rearrange the kinematic equation to solve for acceleration: $$a=\frac{{v_f}^2-{v_i}^2}{2d}$$. Substitute the known values into the equation.
